ceo_biography: Ms. Ami Badani serves as Chief Marketing Officer at ARM. Ami is an accomplished technology leader with over 20 years of experience in marketing, product management, business development, strategy, and investment research. Currently, she serves as the Chief Marketing Officer at Arm, where she leads the global marketing organization, driving company and product positioning, brand awareness, thought leadership, and developer engagement. Before joining Arm, Ami was the Vice President of Marketing and Developer Products at NVIDIA, where she contributed to the company's threefold growth and helped it reach a market cap of three trillion dollars. She was instrumental in driving the data strategy for Generative AI and leading the product, developer ecosystem, and marketing efforts for the data center portfolio, the fastest-growing business segment. Ami also held various roles at Cumulus Networks (acquired by NVIDIA), Instart Logic (acquired by Akamai Technologies), Cisco, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, among others. She holds an MBA from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business and a B.S. in business from the University of Southern California.

name: Ecopoint Medical
description: Echopoint Medical is a medical device company, spun out from University College London (UCL) in 2018, that is developing solutions for cardiovascular diagnostics. The company was founded by a multidisciplinary team of engineers and clinicians: Professor Adrien Desjardins, Dr. Richard Colchester, Professor Ioannis Papakonstantinou, and Dr. Malcolm Finlay, a Consultant Cardiologist at Barts Heart Centre. The founders' journey began with research at UCL, exploring how to integrate fibre optic senso
photonics_rationale: Echopoint Medical demonstrates strong photonics relevance through its core use of fibre optic sensors for cardiovascular diagnostics. The company's foundation on fibre optics technology (optical fibres and sensors) and its spinout from UCL's photonics/biophotonics research group under Professor Desjardins (known for optical coherence tomography research) indicates photonics is central to their medical device platform. While the application is medical/healthcare rather than photonics manufacturing, the technology dependence on integrated fibre optic sensing systems places them solidly in the biophotonics domain.

name: SPARTA Biodiscovery
description: SPARTA Biodiscovery is a spin-out from Imperial College London, founded in 2022 by Professor Dame Molly Stevens and Dr. Jelle Penders, to commercialize a novel nanoparticle analysis platform. The company's creation was a direct result of years of research within the Stevens Group at the university, addressing a pressing need for more advanced analytical tools in the development of next-generation pharmaceuticals. Dr. Penders, who serves as the CEO, co-invented the core technology and spearheaded
photonics_rationale: SPARTA Biodiscovery is primarily a biotech/pharmaceuticals company focused on nanoparticle analysis for drug development. While nanoparticle characterization may employ optical/photonic techniques (such as spectroscopy or light-scattering methods), photonics is not their core identity or primary value proposition—the company's focus is analytical platform development for pharma. No direct evidence of photonics patents, photonics-specific technology development, or photonics industry classification is apparent. The score reflects minor photonics technology usage (analytical techniques may use optical methods) but weak overall photonics relevance.

pitchbook_deals.synopsis: A minory stake in the company was acquired by Sartorius for GBP 3.5 million of development capital on October 23, 2023. The funds will be used to help Sartorius customers optimise the design of nanoparticles at an early stage, even before the drug candidates move on to the clinic.
The company was spun out of the Imperial College London in 2022.
The company joined Imperial Enterprise Lab on an undisclosed date. No equity or funding was exchanged as a result of this program.

ceo_biography: Mr. Jelle Penders is a Co-Founder and serves as Chief Executive Officer and Chief Technology Officer at SPARTA Biodiscovery. Experienced postdoctoral research associate in the field of biomaterials and analytical chemistry. Co-inventor of SPARTA and led the technical development since its inception, has a strong background in nano characterisation techniques including Raman spectroscopy and Electron Microscopy. He is driven to build a sustainable business around the SPARTA® technology, with patient benefit at its heart, to catalyze the full potential of the next generation of safe and effective nanomedicine treatments.

name: COMIND TECHNOLOGIES LIMITED
description:
CoMind is a pioneering company in the field of neurotechnology, focusing on the development of advanced neuromonitoring devices and neural interfaces. These technologies aim to revolutionize patient care by providing doctors with access to a wide range of critical neurophysiological signals that are currently difficult to measure. The company serves healthcare providers, particularly those specializing in neurology and brain disorders, by offering non-invasive solutions that optimize patient outcomes across the care continuum.

Operating in the healthcare and medical devices market, CoMind employs a business model centered around the research, development, and commercialization of its innovative products. Revenue is generated through the sale of these neuromonitoring devices to hospitals, clinics, and research institutions globally. The company also collaborates with medical professionals and institutions to ensure the practical application and continuous improvement of its technologies.

CoMind's team comprises experts in non-invasive neuroimaging, neuroscience, and biomedical engineering, all committed to advancing the field of neurotechnology. The company is dedicated to fostering an inclusive environment that promotes collaboration and idea sharing across multiple disciplines, driving innovation and impactful product development.

Keywords: neurotechnology, neuromonitoring, neural interfaces, non-invasive, neurophysiological signals, patient care, healthcare, medical devices, neuroscience, biomedical engineering.
photonics_rationale: CoMind has a strong photonics identity centered on proprietary optical sensor technology and laser systems for non-invasive neural monitoring. The company's flagship product explicitly uses low-power lasers and infrared light technology as core value propositions, placing photonics (specifically biophotonics and optical sensing) at the heart of their neuromonitoring devices. Multiple evidence sources confirm this: dealroom tags reference 'optical sensors', the technology description highlights 'photonics system' and 'laser light', and the product uses infrared sensors for measuring cerebral parameters—all representing clear biophotonics applications within the medical optics domain.
